title: WordPress Recovery Mode Workflow
description: Admin receives recovery email, follows secure link, and manages PHP error recovery sessions. Introduced in WordPress 5.2 as the Error Protection API.
actors: Site Admin (receives email, clicks recovery link, manages session)
WordPress System (detects fatal errors, orchestrates recovery lifecycle)

use_cases.name: Detect Fatal Error
use_cases.trigger: WP_Fatal_Error_Handler detects PHP fatal error during request
use_cases.notes: Identifies error source (plugin/theme via file path). Skips network-activated plugins. On non-protected endpoints returns WP_Error without emailing.

use_cases.name: Send Recovery Mode Email
use_cases.trigger: Fatal error on protected endpoint, recovery mode not yet active
sub_steps: Check rate limit via option 'recovery_mode_email_last_sent' (default: 1 day)
Resolve recipient: RECOVERY_MODE_EMAIL constant or admin_email option
use_cases.notes: Rate-limited. Returns WP_Error 'email_sent_already' if within rate limit window.

use_cases.name: Generate Recovery URL
sub_steps: key_service->generate_and_store_recovery_mode_key($token) → hashes key with PasswordHash, stores in DB option 'recovery_keys'
use_cases.notes: Only one valid URL at a time. Key is consumed (deleted) on use. URL filtered via 'recovery_mode_begin_url'.

use_cases.name: Enter Recovery Mode via Link
sub_steps: Validate page is wp-login.php with correct GET params
key_service->validate_recovery_mode_key($token, $key, $ttl): checks existence, hash match, expiry; removes key (one-time use)
cookie_service->set_cookie(): generates base64(recovery_mode|iat|rand|hmac), sets cookie for WEEK_IN_SECONDS

use_cases.name: Validate Recovery Session Cookie
sub_steps: parse_cookie(): base64_decode, split on '|' into 4 parts
Check created_at is numeric and within WEEK_IN_SECONDS
Verify HMAC signature using AUTH_KEY+AUTH_SALT (or site options fallback)
use_cases.notes: Invalid/expired cookie: clear_cookie() + wp_die(403). Cookie uses recovery-mode-specific HMAC, not wp_hash(), loaded before plugins.

use_cases.name: Manage Error in Active Recovery Session
use_cases.trigger: Fatal error occurs while recovery mode is active (is_active=true)
sub_steps: store_error(): identify extension, call wp_paused_plugins()->set() or wp_paused_themes()->set()
redirect_protected(): wp_safe_redirect() to reload page with extension paused

use_cases.name: Exit Recovery Mode
use_cases.trigger: Admin clicks 'Exit Recovery Mode' link or logs out

use_cases.name: Clean Expired Recovery Keys
use_cases.notes: Removes keys where time() > created_at + ttl. TTL defaults to email rate limit (1 day), filterable via 'recovery_mode_email_link_ttl'.

key_classes.name: WP_Recovery_Mode_Email_Service
key_classes.role: Email delivery with rate limiting (final class)

business_rules: Recovery email rate-limited to once per day (filterable)
Recovery link TTL must be >= email rate limit; defaults to 1 day
Recovery key is single-use: deleted immediately upon validation
Cookie valid for 1 week (WEEK_IN_SECONDS), filterable via 'recovery_mode_cookie_length'
Network-activated plugins are excluded from recovery mode handling
Only errors from plugins/themes trigger recovery (not core WordPress errors)
Error must occur on a 'protected endpoint' to trigger email flow
Exiting recovery mode clears all paused plugin/theme records
Cookie HMAC uses AUTH_KEY+AUTH_SALT or auto-generated site options fallback (recovery_mode_auth_key, recovery_mode_auth_salt)
wp_filters_and_actions: Filter: recovery_mode_begin_url — modify recovery begin URL
Filter: recovery_email_support_info — support text in email
Filter: recovery_email_debug_info — debug info array in email
Action: generate_recovery_mode_key — fires when key is generated
